sectiondef.header: Variable names for free data folder structure

briefdescription.para: Starts the Async Framework with numThreads parallel threads.

parameterdescription.para: number of threads to setup for processing data, must be >= 1 and <= ASYNC_MAX_THREADS
[optional, default = 0] when set to 1 the background task processing readouts is not started

simplesect.para: 1 if ASYNC framework was started, 0 if ASYNC framework was already running, in this case the number of threads is not changed

briefdescription.para: Prototype function for an async worker function.

parameterdescription.para: reference to thread data folder

simplesect.para: data folder reference to output data folder

briefdescription.para: Prototype function for an async readout function.

parameterdescription.para: reference to returned data folder from thread
error code, only set in the error case
error message, only set in the error case

briefdescription.para: thread function that receives data folders from the thread input queue and calls the setup custom worker function. With :cpp:func:ASYNC_Start numThreads :cpp:func:ASYNC_Thread functions will be started in their own thread. Each will run until the framework is stopped. UTF_NOINSTRUMENTATION
Receives data from finished workloads. Calls the user defined readout function. For in order readouts this function buffers pending result data folders until they can be processed in order.
Allows to check if all executed work loads of a specific work load class were read out. For that case, the work load class can optionally be removed from Async.

parameterdescription.para: work load class string
[optional, default = 0] when set the specified work load class is removed from Async The parameter has no effect, if the work loads of the specified class are not done.

simplesect.para: 1 if work load class finished, 0 if work load class did not finish, NaN if work load class not known to ASYNC

briefdescription.para: Wait for TP analysis from specific panel to finish and remove it.

parameterdescription.para: name of work load class
time out in seconds

simplesect.para: 0 if work load class is unknown or has finished and was removed, 1 if timeout was encountered.

briefdescription.para: Background function that reads data folders from the thread output queue and calls the setup custom readout function The function takes care to buffer results if they should be processed in order.

parameterdescription.para: default structure for Igor background tasks

memberdef.argsstring: (dfref dfr, wave w=defaultValue, variable var=defaultValue, string str=defaultValue, variable move=defaultValue, string name=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: ASYNC_AddParam

briefdescription.para: Adds one parameter to a data folder for a threaded worker execution.

detaileddescription.para: The parameters are globals in the threads data folder and named param0, param1, param2... in order of adding them. Only one parameter can be added per call. move is only allowed in combination with a wave.

parameterdescription.para: data folder reference to thread df, prepared by calling ASYNC_PrepareDF
[optional, default = null] wave reference of wave to be added as parameter
[optional, default = 0] variable to be added as parameter
[optional, default = 0] string to be added as parameter
[optional, default = 0] if a wave was given as parameter and move is not zero then the wave is moved to the threads data folder
[optional, default = paramXXX] name of the added parameter

briefdescription.para: Fetch a wave from the DFREF in the worker function.
Fetch a variable from the DFREF in the worker function.
Fetch a string from the DFREF in the worker function.
Stops the Async Framework.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, default = Inf] time in s to wait for running threads, the function waits for pending threads and pending readouts within this time. After a timeout happened the readout processing is stopped and threads are stopped. Any pending data is lost. If the threads have to be stopped forcefully, an assertion is raised. Using a finite timeout is strongly recommended.
[optional, default = 0] specified when called as cleanup function from ASSERT Suppresses further assertions such that all required cleanup routines such as ThreadGroupRelease are executed.

simplesect.para: 2 if ASYNC framework was not running, 1 if a timeout was encountered, 0 otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (string WorkerFunc, string ReadOutFunc, string workloadClass, variable inOrder=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: ASYNC_PrepareDF

briefdescription.para: Prepares a thread data folder for use with the AsyncFramework This function should be called first when setting up a worker with parameters.

parameterdescription.para: string naming a threadsafe worker function in the form of the ASYNC_Worker template
string naming a readout function in the form of the ASYNC_ReadOut template
string naming a work load class for work load attribution like "TestPulse" The string must follow strict object naming rules.
[optional, default = 1] flag that allows to disable in order readout of results

simplesect.para: data folder for thread, where parameters can be put to with :cpp:func:ASYNC_AddParam

briefdescription.para: Puts a prepared thread data folder to parallel execution in another thread.

parameterdescription.para: data folder that is setup for thread and is to be deployed

briefdescription.para: Returns 1 if var is a finite/normal number, 0 otherwise.
Returns one if str is empty or null, zero otherwise.

detaileddescription.para:
str
must not be a SVAR
UTF_NOINSTRUMENTATION

briefdescription.para: Returns 1 if var is a NaN, 0 otherwise.
Checks if the datafolder referenced by dfr exists.

detaileddescription.para:
Unlike DataFolderExists() a dfref pointing to an empty ("") dataFolder is considered non-existing here.
one if dfr is valid and references an existing or free datafolder, zero otherwise Taken from http://www.igorexchange.com/node/2055 UTF_NOINSTRUMENTATION

briefdescription.para: Return a nicely formatted multiline stacktrace.
Low overhead function to check assertions.

detaileddescription.para:
var
if zero an error message is printed into the history and procedure execution is aborted, nothing is done otherwise. If the debugger is enabled, it also steps into it.
errorMsg
error message to output in failure case
Example usage: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * .. code-block:: igorpro * * ControlInfo/W = $device popup_MoreSettings_DeviceType * ASSERT(V_flag > 0, "Non-existing control or window") * do something with S_value *

briefdescription.para: Low overhead function to check assertions (threadsafe variant)

detaileddescription.para:
var
if zero an error message is printed into the history and procedure execution is aborted, nothing is done otherwise.
errorMsg
error message to output in failure case
Example usage: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * .. code-block:: igorpro * * ASSERT(DataFolderExistsDFR(dfr), "MyFunc: dfr does not exist") * do something with dfr *
Unlike ASSERT() this function does not print a stacktrace or jumps into the debugger. The reasons are Igor Pro limitations. Therefore it is advised to prefix errorMsg with the current function name.

briefdescription.para: Check wether the function reference points to the prototype function or to an assigned function.

detaileddescription.para: Due to Igor Pro limitations you need to pass the function info from FuncRefInfo and not the function reference itself.

simplesect.para: 1 if pointing to prototype function, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Returns the full path to a global variable.

parameterdescription.para: location of the global variable, must exist
name of the global variable
initial value of the variable. Will only be used if it is created. 0 by default.

briefdescription.para: Returns the full path to a global string.

parameterdescription.para: location of the global string, must exist
name of the global string
initial value of the string. Will only be used if it is created. null by default.

briefdescription.para: Create a datafolder and all its parents,.

detaileddescription.para:
Includes fast handling of the common case that the datafolder exists.
reference to the datafolder

briefdescription.para: Returns string path to async framework home data folder.
Returns reference to async framework home data folder.
test if data folder is marked for thread usage
Returns string path to the thread group id.
Returns string path to the number of threads.
Returns string path to flag if background task was disabled.

Return wave reference to wave with data folder reference buffer for delayed readouts 1d wave for data folder references, starts with size 0 when jobs should be read out in order, the waiting data folders are buffered in this wave e.g. if the next read out would be job 2, but a data folder from job 3 is returned the data folder is buffered until the one from job 2 appears from the output queue.

Returns wave ref for workload tracking 2d wave row stores work load classes named through dimension label column 0 stores how many work loads were pushed to Async column 1 stores how many work loads were read out from Async.
Returns wave ref for buffering results when THREADING_DISABLED is defined 1D wave using NOTE_INDEX logic.
returns 1 if ASYNC framework is running, 0 otherwise

Deletes one row, column, layer or chunk from a wave Advantages over DeletePoints: Keeps the dimensionality of the wave when deleting the last row, column, layer or chunk in a wave Implements range check Advantages over DeletePoints + KillWaves: The wave reference stays valid.

parameterdescription.para: wave where the row, column, layer or chunk should be deleted
dimension 0 - rows, 1 - column, 2 - layer, 3 - chunk
index where one point in the given dimension is deleted

briefdescription.para: Check if a name for an object adheres to the strict naming rules.
ASYNC This file holds the asynchronous execution framework

para.verbatim: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * See :ref:`async_framework_doc` for the full documentation. *
